title: Spear Phishing as a Violation of the Domain Knowledge Graph — reproducibility package
description:
Reproducibility package for the paper "Spear Phishing as a Violation of the Domain Knowledge Graph: Cascade Dynamics and Leak-Aware Evaluation" (Warpechowski & Księżopolski).

Modern email filters catch mass phishing (~98%), but the residual spear phishing and Business Email Compromise (BEC) slip through precisely because they respect the victim's domain knowledge. We model that knowledge as a multiplex graph. The empirically strongest signal is temporal cascade dynamics — a receiving-side wave that survives stealthy attacks and replicates on three real graphs — while cross-layer inconsistency is shown synthetically as a proof of soundness. We also measure organizational susceptibility and define detection effectiveness under a leak-aware protocol.

This archive contains: the LaTeX manuscript and figures (paper/), the experiment and analysis code (code/), the generated synthetic population of 150 personas (code/data/twins/), and all computed experiment outputs (code/results/) from which the tables and figures reproduce. The large public input datasets (Enron maildir, SNAP graphs) are not redistributed; download pointers are in code/README.md. Third-party reference PDFs are intentionally excluded for copyright reasons.

creators.name: Warpechowski, Kamil
creators.affiliation: Kozminski University; NASK National Research Institute, Warsaw, Poland
